evidence.scientific_clarity: Dual-axis semilogy loss (left/blue) + dashed cosine LR (right/red) exactly matches brief; axis ticks color-coupled to lines.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans — the matplotlib default, not editable or selectable.
evidence.palette: Blue/red pair avoids the problematic red-green combination and is largely CB-safe, but red is still difficult for protanopes; directional discipline handled via caption note.
evidence.layout_density: figsize=(6.5,3.8) with tight_layout() and xlim clipped to data range; no gaping whitespace or label overlap observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption explicitly flags opposite-trend axes per brief hint; legend placed upper-right; caption values match script output.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts shows 'BMQQDV+DejaVuSans Type 3' — bitmap fonts, not Type 42 TrueType; text is not selectable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (16 kB), figure.png (66 kB), caption.tex (432 B), gen_figure.py (1.6 kB) all present and non-trivial; bonus figure.svg also saved.
evidence.caption_quality: Quantifies loss drop (~2.19 to ~0.21) and LR range (10^-3 to 0), explains color/axis mapping, and warns against directional cross-axis comparison.

overall_strengths: Caption is exemplary: numerical claims, color-axis mapping, and the directional-comparison caveat all present.
Layout is clean and compact — tight_layout, color-coupled axis labels, and sensible figure dimensions.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 never set, producing Type 3 bitmap fonts in the PDF; text is not selectable or editable by a journal production system.
Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout; switching to a serif or a publication-standard sans (e.g. Helvetica/Arial via rcParams) would improve typography score.
